task: Design a JSON schema for a multi-tenant SaaS billing system supporting usage-based and seat-based pricing

criteria.description: Schema covers all required entities and pricing models
criteria.pass_condition: Entities: tenant, plan, subscription, usage_record, invoice, line_item. Pricing models: per-seat, usage-based (metered), tiered, hybrid. Billing cycles, proration, trial periods.

sub_attributes.measurement: % of required entities modeled (tenant, plan, subscription, usage, invoice, line_item)
1.0 if both + hybrid, 0.75 if both, 0.5 if one, 0.0 if neither
% of lifecycle events modeled (create, upgrade, downgrade, cancel, prorate, trial)

fail_examples: Just 'plan' and 'subscription' with flat price field

criteria.description: Valid JSON Schema with proper types, constraints, references
criteria.pass_condition: Valid JSON Schema (draft-07+). Uses $ref for reuse. Required fields marked. Enums for constrained values. Proper date-time formats.

sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if valid draft-07+, 0.5 if mostly valid, 0.0 if invalid
1.0 if DRY with $ref, 0.5 if some reuse, 0.0 if duplicated

pass_examples: $ref to shared 'money' type with currency+amount, enum for billing_interval
fail_examples: Freeform JSON with no type constraints

criteria.description: Schema is extensible without breaking changes
criteria.pass_condition: Uses additionalProperties judiciously. Versioned. Metered dimensions are a list (not hardcoded). Custom metadata fields supported.

sub_attributes.description: Usage dimensions are dynamic, not hardcoded
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if array of dimension objects, 0.5 if a few named fields, 0.0 if hardcoded
1.0 if metadata object on key entities, 0.0 if closed

sub_attributes.description: Schema version field or versioning strategy

pass_examples: metered_dimensions: [{id, unit, tiers: [...]}] — add new dimensions without schema change

criteria.description: Schema reflects real billing system patterns (Stripe-informed, not academic)
criteria.pass_condition: Models concepts from real systems: idempotency keys, invoice status lifecycle, webhook events, currency handling. Avoids naive patterns (storing calculated totals without line items).
pass_examples: Invoice with status enum (draft→open→paid→void), line_items array, currency code per amount
fail_examples: Invoice with just 'total: 99.99' and no line items
